title: Recover Missing HTML
description: Open Bloom, select a book, close Bloom, delete the .htm file (NOT the bookhtml.bak file), and restart Bloom. Bloom should recover and display a warning to the user. Message says: "Bloom had a problem reading this book and recovered by restoring a recent backup. Please check recent changes to this book. If this happens for no obvious reason, please report it to us."
stepDescription: Open book, delete .htm, restart Bloom, verify recovery.
checklistSteps: Delete the .htm file, but leave bookhtml.bak in place.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom recovers, shows a warning, and restores from a recent backup.

title: TEST SETUP There are three permissions
description: TEST SETUP: There are three permissions categories on Bloom Library: Regular user, Collection Editor, and Moderator. To test, you will need an account from all three categories. The following presumes you have Collection Editor permission for the "Pratham" bookshelf.

title: Badges Book thumbnail badges indicating Bloom
description: Badges: Book thumbnail badges indicating Bloom Library status. There are four statuses seen here. 1) uploaded & harvested 2) waiting for harvester 3) uploaded as draft 4) uploaded and removed from publication. Completion of harvesting -- movement from State (2) to (1) or (2) to 3) -- will only be shown after re-booting Bloom. Likewise, state (4) is only detected after initial re-boot (or changing collections). Test bulk upload.

title: MY BOOKS Sign as regular user
description: MY BOOKS: Sign in as a regular user, click on the top right avatar, and choose My Books. Click on any book. You should have see "You have permission to modify this book". Go to any book not in "My Books" --> the permissions are hidden.

title: DOWNLOAD INTO BLOOM EDITING When logged
description: DOWNLOAD INTO BLOOM FOR EDITING:  When logged in as a Regular user, the "download for editing" button should only be available for "My Books" and no others; for a Collection Editor, the button is present for all books in the collection, or "My Books" (but no others); for a Moderator, all books have the button.

title: Click Download Editing button new instance
description: - Click Download for Editing button. A new instance of Bloom will open. The thumbnail should show the downloaded badge. The "sources for new books" should be hidden. Collection settings should match the original settings of the uploaded book including Enterprise settings. The name of the collection should be "From Bloom Library - NAME OF BOOK". The branding should be maintained. You should not be able to create a new book in the collection.

title: Update Books without losing branding make
description: Update Books without losing branding: make a book with any current project branding and upload it then download it for editing. A new instance of Bloom opens. Verify branding is maintained in the book. Add a new page and re-upload the updated book (tick "yes"). Verify branding is retained on Blorg and new page has been added. Add another page. Go to settings Enterprise and change the branding to None. Upload to web again. This time the "yes" option should require you to tick a box to acknowledge the branding is changing to None. Tick both and upload. Verify branding for the book on Blorg. Change branding to None and reupload. Verify you have to acknowledge the change in branding. Upload and verify result on Blorg.

title: Changing ownership Log as Moderator download
description: Changing ownership: Log in as a Moderator and download a book for editing from the Pratham bookshelf which is not owned by either Moderator, Collection Editor, or Regular user. After Bloom opens, go to Publish to Web, tick agreements, and click upload. Under the "yes" option, the collision dialog should present tick box to change ownership. Don't upload. Close Bloom and log out as moderator and log in a Collection Editor. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should allow you to change ownership. Don't upload. Close Bloom and log out as Collection Editor and log in a Regular user. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should not present the option to change ownership and should not allow you to upload (i.e. after clicking "yes", the "upload" button should remain greyed out). Close Bloom and log out as Regular user and log in a Collection Editor. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should allow you to change ownership. Tick boxes and upload. Verify on Blorg that ownership has changed. Try re-uploading. This time the change-ownership box won't appear because owership has been transferred.

description: In Enterprise Settings, enter  Sample-727011-1960. Text and images should show in 7 slots. PDF. Note, the PDF is wrong for the Title page. On 23 Feb 2022, the Sample branding was changed so that page has a single Little Zebra Books logo on the bottom of the title page.

title: Choose ONE particular branding examine following
description:
Choose ONE particular branding and examine the following:

- Check logos appear correctly in the 1) Edit view 2) PDF 3) EPUB 4) Bloom Reader.  See the "Branding Screenshots" sheet in this file. [See settings/Bloom Enterprise. See the folder browser/branding/X for hints on what should appear for each branding. Images should appear where their names suggest. Json settings should be applied to new books.]  The brandings should be checked in generated books, not just in the Bloom preview. See notes at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1IRwsfHnvmR0AnhiJFgp8EOrFAhXmF1U3tOGe-Y7p2oA/edit?usp=sharing. Remember to set the appropriate xmatter, if the branding has a custom one associated with it. (JT 6.3: as far as I can find in the subscriptions doc, there is no longer a current Kyrgyz branding. This makes it difficult to test all the things in the notes. I tried the Little Zebra branding, which has obvious custom stuff on the bottom of the front cover, the bottom of the title page, and the top of the outside back cover. It does not, however, have hyperlinks.)

title: Outside back cover image appear page
description: - Ensure Outside back cover image appear on page thumbnail. N.B. we won't fix BL-9010.

title: Applying global image metadata changes clobber
description: - Applying global image metadata changes should not clobber metadata of branding images. N.B. currently, we won't fix this.

title: Make sure expired enterprise subscriptions e
description: - Make sure expired enterprise subscriptions (e.g. Juarez-Guatemala-003686-8686) give message "That code has expired"

title: you can copy paste enterprise codes
description: - Ensure you can copy and paste enterprise codes.

title: Set ABC branding Ensure Grade level
description: Set to ABC branding. Ensure Grade level and "Level Letters" are working. (See note.) (JT 6.3.3028: there is no longer an ABC subscription, so this can't fully be done. I used download-for-edit to get an ABC book, and the "Grade 1" in the top right of the cover is present. I found that a Grade 3 book is immediately converted to grade 1 when edited, both in 6.3 and 6.1. I don't see any current way to create a new ABC book, so some of the things in Notes can't be tried. I don't know what "Level Letters" means.)

title: Changing Enterprise subscription Locally funded vice
description: Changing from Enterprise subscription to Locally funded or vice-versa. Ensure the various problems outlined in BL-11284 do not happen. (JT 6.3: there's no longer a single Locally funded subscription. I tried the Tuki-Community one. The bug appears to be about our logo overflowing on the last page; I did not observe this.)

title: Download some books Ensure 2nd instance
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- Download some books. Ensure a 2nd instance of Bloom does not open, even on the second or later books downloaded while a first instance is open.

title: translating downloaded shell
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- Try translating a downloaded shell

title: Can be done one two three
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- Can this be done with one, two, and three languages? If so test this.

title: Look corners different kinds xmatter Can
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- Look for corners in (different kinds of) xmatter. Can each field that should be translatable be translated?

title: What different fields be editable locked
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- What different fields should be editable and locked in a translation vs original authoring? May vary by xMatter pack. Irrelevant 5.5 onwards

title: Check fields buttons be disabled while
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- Check fields/buttons that should be disabled while translating (e.g. images). Does the toolbox:gear:allow changes switch  enable those fields to be edited? Update: this is now the lock icon in the lower left. Irrelevant 5.5 onwards

title: be able click see different source
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- Should be able to click to see different source languages (for example, in a derivative of Moon and Cap, a source Bubble shows Tibetan, French, and English. Any of these should be selectable and result in showing source text in that language.

title: If source language is set as
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- If a source language is set as a national language and you choose "two languages" (bilingual mode) it should move from the source bubble to the page

title: Close Bloom Download book Blorg When
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Close Bloom. Download a book from Blorg. When download is complete and you click OK, Bloom should open and present that book for translation.

title: you can copy translation bubble N
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Ensure you can copy a translation bubble. [N.B. we are willing to live with BL-10294.]

title: Test selecting different pages book many
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Test selecting different pages for a book with many pages: Select a new page. Ensure list doesn't jump (BL-11528). Try dragging the scroll bar to move thumbnails. Observe memory performance.

title: Create book special unique font which
description: Create a book with a special/unique font which Harvester knows about.  The Harvester should succeed, and the book should be able to be read online with that font.  Downloaded ePUB and BloomPub books should contain the font so that they can be read with it.  If the font was used in a style, then the style in a downloaded book should still refer to the font even if hasn't been installed.  [Noto Sans Tamil from Google fonts is one that Harvester knows about.  Scheherazade New from SIL is another such font.]

title: Create book special unique font which
description: Create a book with a special/unique font which Harvester does not know about. The Harvester should fail and Blorg should show a message like this, but the PDF should work.

title: Verify book thumbnail Library is just
description: Verify that the book thumbnail on Library is just the image of the front cover. [JT: I think it's sufficient to upload a book (to our 'sandbox', dev.bloomlibrary.org), follow the link to the resulting book, and check that the thumbnail just look like the front cover picture. For example, it doesn't have the title (not on  inside the rectangle that has the cove-color background, anyway or any of the other text, and the size and shape match the picture rather than the whole cover.]

title: Create custom thumbnail 256 png book
description: Create a "custom-thumbnail-256.png" for a book and upload to Bloom Library. (Dimensions must be exactly 256x256 pixels.) When the book is harvested, the custom thumbnail should show up, rather than a portion of the cover image.

title: all modes PDF publishing A5 book
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Try all the modes of PDF publishing for an A5 book.

title: Check correct PDF generation each page
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Check correct PDF generation for each page layout (portrait & landscape), including page numbers. Ensure thumbnails are correct.

title: New Moon Cap change layout A5Portrait
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

New Moon and Cap, change layout from A5Portrait to A5Landscape, Publish simple PDF

title: Download one Look Listen Live books
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Download one of Look, Listen and Live books. Publish simple PDF. Compare this PDF with the PDF on Blorg and ensure they are exactly the same. Pay close attention to margins. (Note that in order to get the font to match you may need to set your collection default font to Calibri)

title: Actually print book see if folds
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Actually print a book: see if it folds properly and makes a book. "Half-letter" makes it easy to test with Letter paper.

title: Change Settings Book Making Page Numbering
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Change the Settings:Book Making:Page Numbering to something non-standard and ensure they are used in the PDF.

title: Booklet PDF generation Legal Landscape works
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Ensure Booklet PDF generation for Legal Landscape works. (Decided to live with this.)

title: Make full bleed book currently by
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Make a full-bleed book (currently: by choosing Kyrgyz branding; Kyrgyz 2020 xmatter should be automatic). Make a full-bleed PDF. Open the output in Acrobat. Paper size should be about 6mm larger than A5 in both directions (154x216)--File/Properties. Turn on the preference in Acrobat (Edit/Preferences/PageDisplay/Show art, trim, & bleed boxes) to see trim lines and check they are 3mm inside page.

title: make sure there are no extra
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- make sure there are no extra or missing pages

title: make simple PDFs cover content booklets
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- make simple PDFs, cover and content booklets with full bleed off. Also try booklets with full bleed on...either they should be disabled or it should make no difference. Check standard A5 and A4 page sizes, no missing pages.

title: make some PDFs CMYK turned s
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- make some PDFs with CMYK turned on. It's difficult to check the output really is CYMK, but at least it should look reasonable and not have obvious problems

title: Settings customize language name Ensure custom
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

In Settings, customize the language name. Ensure this custom name appears 1) on the front cover, 2) on the # of books in Bloom Library banner, and 3) is part of the proposed file name when saving the PDF to disk.

title: Click every link ePUB preview screen
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Click every link in the ePUB preview screen

title: Create epub Ensure padding react progress
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Create an epub. Ensure padding in react progress dialogue.

title: Check which font files font faces
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Check which font files and font faces are added to the epub. Bold and Italic should be included. (An ePub is just a zip file. Unzip and look in "fonts" folder.)

title: at least one epub phone make
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Try at least one epub on a phone; make sure it looks right and audio plays  (If you don't have the Simply Reading app, Infinity Reader will play audio automagically if you hold the phone right.)  Dolphin EasyReader can play embedded audio, but looks awful.  Even it tries to use TTS instead of embedded audio at times.  Other readers all seem to use only TTS and ignore the embedded audio.

title: Set settings book making special script
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Set the settings:book making:special script settings to Right to Left. Export to epub and verify that things look right, including: navigation arrows work opposite direction. Note: "not addressed hiding the inapplicable button when showing the first or last page".

title: only fonts local national language are
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Ensure that only fonts for the local and national language are embedded when the regional (L3) language is never used in the book, and that fonts assigned to styles are not embedded if the styles aren't used in the final book. (and conversely that all fonts needed are embedded for all three languages and for customized styles.)  Currently, this is a bug we're willing to live with.

title: Test summary description epub metadata is
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Test summary/description in epub metadata is same as "summary" in BLib. publish. Ensure editing & mouse movements work ok.

title: lots collections books AppData Local SIL
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

With lots of collections and books in AppData\Local\SIL\Bloom\Collections, compare performance of epub generation with older versions of Bloom and ensure there's no performance regression

title: Use combo imported Bloom recorded audio
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Use a combo of imported and Bloom-recorded audio on one page (or two imported). Adjust Timings on both. Make sure epub preview audio plays correctly.

title: N B sidebar which lists Text
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

N.B. The sidebar which lists Text Languages, Talking Book Languages, Features, and Cover Color should behave the same between BloomPUB and Web publishing -- settings should be mirrored and remembered when switching between one and the other or exiting Bloom. Be sure to test this!

title: Text Languages Ensure 1 all content
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Text Languages: Ensure 1) all content languages are presented and your selection is remembered if you exit publish or switch between BloomPUB and Web Publish; 2) checkboxes are disabled and checked for any "required" languages regardless of completeness (e.g. a trilingual book will have three checked languages which are greyed out); 3) only the languages selected are uploaded (web) or embedded in saved BloomPUB; 4) tooltip says "Select this if you want readers to be able to choose to read the book in this language"; 5) if the book is bilingual or trilingual, then those languages cannot be unchecked regardless of completeness. 6) incomplete languages are correctly identified ; 7) a language which is only in xmatter is not advertised on blorg [Quick test resource: download this book in an English collection and publish. There should be English, French, Hausa, Spanish & Swahili.]

title: Talking Book Languages Ensure 1 all
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Talking Book Languages: Ensure 1) all audio languages embedded in the book are presented and are checked by default and your selection is remembered if you exit publish or switch between BloomPUB and Web Publish; 2) all boxes can be unchecked and; 3) tooltip says "Select this if you want to include this audio"; 4) the checkmark beside Features: Talking Book is checked iff there is one audio language checked. [Quick test: download this book into an English collection and publish. There should audio in English & Tok Pisin.] 5) Talking Book Language X is advertised/listed under Talking Book Languages if and only if: there exists some text in language X in at least one content/interior page of the book
6) Talking Book Language X is included in a BloomPUB if and only if:  language X is selected in the list of Talking Book Languages.

title: Sign Language if Sign Language is
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Sign Language: if a Sign Language is set in Settings:Languages, but the book has no video, then: 1) the SL feature should be greyed out 2) the tooltip should say "This is disabled because this book does not have any videos." Upload to Bloom Library and ensure SL icon does not appear.

title: Sign Language if Sign Language is
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Sign Language: if a Sign Language is set in Settings:Languages, and the book has a video, then: 1) the SL feature should be checked by default and 2) the tooltip should identify the sign language in a hyperlink. Click on the hyperlink and change the SL to something else. Ensure this takes and propagates to the collection settings. Upload the book to Bloom Library with SL feature checked and ensure SL icon appears. Upload the same book with SL feature unchecked and ensure video is uploaded but the book is not tagged for SL (SL icon does not appear). (Hint: easier, choose 'these are different books in the upload dialog to create two separate books and compare).

title: Sign Language if Sign Language is
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Sign Language: if a Sign Language is not set in Settings:Languages, and the book does have video, then the 1) SL feature should be greyed out by default and 2) the tooltip should say "This is disabled because this collection has no sign language selected. Click on the hyperlink in the tooltip should enable you to choose a sign language after which the box will be checked by default and the SL will be populated in Settings:Language (verify). Upload book and verify it is tagged as a Sign Language book.

title: Game Turn Enterprise feature be checked
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Game: Turn Enterprise on. This feature should be checked iff there is at least one game page. Tool tip says "This book has games." Turn Enterprise off. Ensure feature is disabled and tool tip says "This feature requires a Bloom subscription tier of at least "Pro"." Turn Enterprise on again. Ensure Games feature is re-enabled. In Edit, delete all Game pages and republish. Ensure Activity feature is disabled and tooltip says "This book does not have any games."

title: Motion make derivative Moon Cap publish
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Motion: make a derivative of Moon & Cap and publish -> Motion is greyed out. Return to Edit, enable Motion on page 1 and 2 and record narration for 1 and 2 and republish -> Motion Book feature is now checked. Upload to Bloom Library with the feature checked (verify results on Blorg: motion icon appears, READing shows landscape & auto page turn) and unchecked (verify results: motion icon does not appear, READing shows portrait book with no auto page turn).

title: Accessible Set L1 language X Make
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Accessible: Set L1 to language X. Make a new book, add a picture page and publish. Ensure: Accessible feature reads "Accessible to the Visually Impaired in X"; it is disabled; tooltip says "This is disabled because this book does not have any image descriptions in the primary language."  Add an image description and re-publish. Ensure: Accessible feature is available but is not checked by default; tooltip says "Select this if you want to list this book as accessible to the visually impaired in the primary language. This only affects how the book is categorized, it does not affect the content of the book." Publish book to web with and without the box checked and verify results.

title: Cover Color change color Web verify
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Cover Color: change color in Web and verify it propagates to BloomPUB in preview; add new custom colors and ensure they are added to quick chooser.

title: Make book Template Starter publish web
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Make a book from the Template Starter and publish to web.

title: READ template ensure icons various types
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- READ the template and ensure icons for the various types of content appear

title: Verify bold italic show up correctly
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Verify that bold & italic show up correctly.

title: Run several tests ensure when re
description: Run several tests to ensure that when re-uploading books to Bloom Library, only files that have changed are uploaded. Note: the PDF is always regenerated and there may be some small Bloom files that get uploaded (don't worry about these), but ensure that heavy-weight items like images, recordings, & videos which have not changed should are not uploaded.

title: test upload re upload book 1000
description: - test upload and re-upload of a book with > 1000 files. After 1st upload, make a small change -- add a page and a picture, then re-upload. [Resource in \Bloom Team\Testing\atomic upload test] The upload should succeed without a message "There was a problem uploading your book. "  Ensure re-upload skips most files, especially all the .mp3 files.

title: Make new moon cap book Change
description: Make a new "moon and cap" book. Change to Device 16x9. Record the title by whole text box and insert a segment marker. Ensure playback retains spacing everywhere Bloom Player is used: BloomPUB preview; BloomPUB Viewer; Bloom Library READ; Bloom Reader.

title: Create book Enterprise only features Turn
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Create a book with Enterprise-only features. Turn off Enterprise. Publishing refuses to open, with a warning message to users.  With Enterprise turned off, you can publish a derivative book with overlays but cannot publish a book with any added overlays (Bloom will show a screen saying Enterprise Required and prompting you to active Enterprise or remove the Overlays).  Other Enterprise pages may allow publishing, but Game pages (at least) are stripped from uploading and BloomPUB creation.  Note that both ePUB and Audio/Video publishing require a Pro subscription regardless of content.

title: enterprise still off remove enterprise only
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

- With enterprise still off, remove the enterprise only pages from the book and publish again. Web and BloomPUB publishing should be allowed without the removal of any pages.  ePUB and Audio/Video publishing require a Pro subscription regardless of content.

title: Publish Bloom Reader via USB via
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Publish to Bloom Reader: via USB and via WiFi.

title: Save bloomPUB file disk then transfer
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Save a .bloomPUB file to disk and then transfer it to a device and make sure it's intact. Also, open the file in BloomPUB Viewer (Windows).

title: only fonts local national language are
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Ensure that only fonts for the local and national language are embedded when the regional (L3) language is never used in the book, and that fonts assigned to styles are not embedded if the styles aren't used in the final book. (and conversely that all fonts needed are embedded for all three languages and for customized styles.)  Note that Andika and ABeeZee are never embedded as they come standard with Bloom Reader.

title: Install new font your computer https
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Install a new font on your computer from https://software.sil.org/fonts/. Create a book using this font and try to publish to Bloom Reader. The font should get embedded.

title: Test Bulk Publishing Google Drive Bloom
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Test Bulk Publishing. In the Google Drive \Bloom Team\Testing\Materials for Bloom Editor Testing, Koen's collection "MC - YRT - Yumi Read Level 3 - ETP.zip" is a good place to start. Ensure resulting .bloombundle can be opened in Bloom Reader.

title: Test page slider Bloom Reader both
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Test the page slider in Bloom Reader with both dragging the slider as well a clicking to a specific spot.

title: When making bloompub strip out files
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

When making a bloompub, strip out files not on a white list

title: Collection L1 different L2 make new
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

In a Collection with L1 different from L2, make a new basic book (or derivative), and put some L2 content in Inside Front Cover, Inside Back Cover, and Outside Back Cover. Ensure that for at least one of them, the L2 text is the only thing on the page (no images). Publish to BloomPUB, preview. Whether or not user has selected L2 in the language settings, the preview should contain all cover pages and their text.

title: Repeat above test Digital Comic Book
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Repeat above test with a Digital Comic Book, this time filling in End Extra 1, 2 & 3.

title: Install older version ensure you get
description: Install an older version and ensure you get an update notice of a new version. Update. Test opening a variety of BloomPUBs downloaded from Blorg. Ensure the correct font is used. See BL-15789.

title: Test Setup 3 Bloom Library accounts
description: Test Setup: 3 Bloom Library accounts are needed: 1) a Regular user (no permissions); 2) a Moderator (all access); 3) a Collection Editor (access to a particular bookshelf called X).

title: Make new book no title no
description:
- Make a new book with no title and no text. Publish Tab Web. Verify both missing title and missing copyright appears. Click to fix copyright --> copyright warning is removed. Click to add title --> Edit opens at Front Cover. Publish Tab Web --> missing title warning remains. Click to fix --> Edit. Add a title. Publish Tab Web --> now the "Agreements" appear. Tick agreements. Sign In as a Regular user. 
If you are uploading to prod (not dev.blorg), make sure to mark DRAFT. Upload book. No collision dialog is presented.

title: Reupload by same user Edit change
description:
- Reupload by same user: in Edit, change title of book and republish it. After clicking the Upload Book button, "Is this an update" dialog appears.
You should see notice that "you-have-permission". Choose "Yes" and click Upload.
After completion, click VIEW ON BLOOM LIBRARY button to verify the new book title appears on Bloom Library.
Verify that Draft status is the same.

title: Reupload by user without permission Bloom
description: - Reupload by user without permission: In Bloom Publish: Web, click to Sign Out. Then Sign In with "Collection Editor" account. After clicking the Upload Book button, the "Is this an update" Dialog should show that you do not have permission and you can't update the book. Click "No, these are different" and click "Upload". Verify that the upload proceeds and verify the book on Blorg.

title: Reupload by user Moderator permission Bloom
description:
- Reupload by user with Moderator permission: In Bloom Edit, change the title once again. In Bloom Publish: Web, Sign Out, then back in with Moderator account. After clicking the Upload Book button, the collision shows you have permission. Ensure the uploader avatar matches the original uploader which is different from the avatar beside "you have permission". Under the "yes" option is a tick box to change the uploader. Do not tick that box. Choose "Yes" and then the UPLOAD button. Verify the uploaded book has the new title and has retained the original uploader.
Verify that Draft status is the same.

title: Reupload Moderator permission time check box
description: - Reupload with Moderator permission and this time check the box to change the uploader. Upload. Verify the uploader change is reflected on Bloom Library.

title: Bloom Library hint using different chrome
description: - On Bloom Library (hint: using a different chrome profile), log in with Moderator permissions and assign the newly uploaded book to the bookshelf for which your Collection Editor alias has permissions.

title: Reupload by user Collection permission Bloom
description: - Reupload by user with Collection permission: In Bloom Edit, change the title once again. In Bloom Publish: Web, click to Sign Out. Then Sign In with Collection Editor alias account. After clicking the Upload Book button, the collision dialog should look something like this. Ensure the uploader avatar matches the correct uploader which is different from the avatar beside "you have permission". Under the "yes" option is a tick box to change the uploader. Do not tick that box. Choose "Yes" and then the UPLOAD button. Verify the uploaded book has the new title and has retained the original uploader.

title: Reupload Collection Editor permission time check
description: - Reupload with Collection Editor permission and this time check the box to change the uploader. Upload. Verify the change in uploader is reflected on Bloom Library.

title: Bulk Upload Quick Test Copy Bulk
description:
Bulk Upload Quick Test
- Copy "Bulk Upload Quick Test" folder from Testing\Materials for Bloom Editor Testing\Bulk Upload Quick Test into Document/Bloom and open it
- update the Bloom Subscription to a newer valid one if necessary; make Bloom Library Bookshelf to be none; delete any of the books if they are still there from the last test pass
- Choose any book, Publish to Web, choose Upload this collection --> warning about needing to set the bookshelf
- set bookshelf and return to upload collection
- confirm results of 4 new books uploaded.
- On Blorg, confirm each according to the screenshots included in the front covers
- Without changing anything, republish collection: -> 4 books skipped
- Change one book, republish --> 1 book updated, 3 books skipped

title: Bloom Enterprise feature Test out Bulk
description: Bloom Enterprise feature. Test out Bulk Upload. Ensure a bookshelf is required. Try multiple uploads to different bookshelves. Ensure correct xmatter is applied, cf. BL-10347 for Kyrgyz project.

title: Set bookshelf none try bulk upload
description: - Set the bookshelf to none and try a bulk upload. Should get a message like this. (JT 6.3: no longer a problem report dialog, the message just appears in the "Upload Progress" area. Note: the upload collection command is found in the pull-down on the right of the "upload book" button.)

title: Collision ID Dialogue Upload book Blorg
description: Collision ID Dialogue: Upload a book to Blorg then upload again. Should get an ID collision dialogue. Test both options.

title: change front cover image above book
description: - change the front cover image of above book and re-upload. The thumbnail on the right-hand side should be the current one.

title: Collision ID Dialogue Upload SL book
description: Collision ID Dialogue: Upload a SL book to Blorg then upload again. SL should be listed in both boxes.

title: Publish book Blorg shift enter its
description: Publish a book to Blorg with shift-enter in its title. Look at the page of the uplaoded book. The shift-enter should be rendered as spaces in the book detail.

title: Secure S3 upload Uploads are now
description:
Secure S3 upload: 
- Uploads are now atomic. If a book is being updated and something causes the process to fail, the book on blorg will remain as it was before the update started." Test this by interrupting (canceling upload or killing Bloom) and ensure the original book remains intact and unchanged.

title: We now only upload files have
description: - We now only upload files that have changed. So audio/video/images which are not altered will not be re-uploaded. Note that .htm files and meta.json files always change and are always uploaded. PDFs are always generated on the fly and always uploaded." Test a couple of different ways: changed audio or video, changed image, deleted pages, etc.

title: Test all aspects upload download ensure
description: - Test all aspects of upload and download to ensure all files are correctly transmitted. This should not affect things like features or languages. After uploading a changed book, download it and inspect it. (JT 6.3: not sure what is meant by not affecting things like features or languages. Presumably if these changed in the updated book, they should change on Blorg, too, after re-uploading. "All aspects" is very broad...that's why we have many lines of test activities for upload. I took it to mean each kind of page element...images, video, widgets.)

title: Upload book over 1000 files Change
description: - Upload a book with over 1000 files. Change one thing -- like crop an image or re-record a sentence -- and reupload. For media files, only the changed image or recording should be reuploaded. (JT 6.3: I used the Tangshang dictionary from Very Large Books subfolder in the Materials for Bloom testing folder. It only has 550 files. Sorry.)

title: Dev We need make sure cleanup
description: - (Dev) "We need to make sure cleanup is happening properly with books which began uploading or updating but didn't finish. Ensure such new books do not show up in blorg in the meantime."

title: drag moves element try all canvas
description: drag moves element: try all canvas element types, including navigation buttons;  try at multiple zoom levels, including 100%, larger, and smaller. Red guide lines should appear when elements are aligned. Movement should snap to a grid (about 10px) unless ctrl is held down.

title: Dragging corner controls resizes bubble try
description: Dragging corner controls resizes bubble: try all bubble types, all corners; try at multiple zoom levels. Most bubbles, including picture buttons, can be resized to any shape; but image and video overlays can only be sized in a way that preserves the aspect ratio of the picture. Again, things should snap to a grid unless ctrl is held down, and red alignment lines should show as appropriate; also green bars when the item being resized is the same height as something else. (Text bubbles are special and do not have corner handles.)

title: width text bubble can be changed
description: The width of a text bubble can be changed using the oval side handles. Normally the height adjusts automatically to fit the text.

title: Turn auto height off using menu
description: Turn auto-height off using the ... menu below a text box. Now there should be a third handle in the middle bottom which allows the height to be manually adjusted. The box will no longer resize automatically as the side handles are moved, nor as text is typed or deleted.

title: Moving one corner bubble cause opposite
description: Moving one corner of a bubble should not cause the opposite corner to move, even slightly. Compare BL-8077, though that was with an older resizing UI.

title: Images but currently image buttons can
description: Images (but not currently image buttons) can be cropped using the handles on the sides. Try all four sides. The image should not move. As you drag outward, the handle should not move beyond the point where the image is no longer cropped. Watch out for rows of narrow lines being left behind during dragging, especially at zoom levels larger than 100%.

title: When image is cropped extra handle
description: When an image is cropped, an extra handle appears, a circle in the middle of the image. This handle allows adjusting the cropping by moving the image, without changing the position or size of the containing box. It should not be possible to move the image beyond any of its edges (i.e., leaving white bars inside the box).

title: cropped image can be resized Resizing
description: A cropped image can be resized. Resizing should preserve the cropping and the cropped aspect ratio.

title: Copy Paste scripture text Paratext project
description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

Copy/Paste scripture text from a Paratext project into a Bloom book to ensure that a) the text is not duplicated; b) superscripts for verse numbers are handled reasonably.
Bloom has code to find "\v N" and convert it to superscript, but this doesn't work when the incoming text is in html because of the markup. In my 6.1-6.3 testing w/PTX 9.5, pasting with Ctrl-V pasted as html and therefore did not give superscripts. Ctrl-Shift-V pasted as text and did.
If superscripts are added, those should be reversible using the standard ckeditor control.

Originally this said: superscripts for verse numbers are formatted as such when copying from "Unformatted" view; otherwise, from "Preview" & "Standard" you get unformatted numbers for verses, and "Formatted" gives backslash codes. Note, "HTML clipboard" prefix is ok.

title: Test right click rename function rename
description:

- Test the right-click rename function. This should rename the book's folder and .htm file, not the title of the book. Can also be started using F2. Subsequent renamings of the title should not affect the renamed folder -- it should be "locked". Right-click rename delete should restore the book back to match the title and unlock the book, i.e. renaming the title should not affect the book folder name. If you make a BloomPub or other publications, the default filename should be based on the folder name, not the title. (JT 6.3: but if you made e.g. a BloomPub previously, Bloom remembers the name you used to make it, and that is the default rather than the current folder name. description:
Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

If you've worked on the book enough to have a backup, it should automatically restore it with a message like this.
